themes: OTA standout battles for TE depth role
New Highmark Stadium ribbon-cutting date set
Bills No. 2 in ESPN Power Index
writeup: OTA practice buzz centered on an under-the-radar tight end making a case for a depth role behind Dalton Kincaid and Dawson Knox. The franchise received a boost in national rankings, landing at No. 2 in ESPN's Football Power Index ahead of the 2026 season. Meanwhile, the organization formally announced the ribbon-cutting date for the new $2.2 billion Highmark Stadium, building anticipation for what promises to be a transformative chapter in Bills history.

articles.title: Can this OTA standout make noise as a deep depth option at TE?
New Highmark Stadium ribbon cutting date announced
Bills at No. 2 in ESPN's Football Power Index
